sae_features.label: S1A trypsin-like catalytic domains
sae_features.description: Extracellular trypsin-like serine protease catalytic domains (S1A family) in metazoan zymogens—capturing the fold-level, glycine/serine‑rich active‑site and substrate‑binding loop architecture (often including the GDSGG segment) of the C‑terminal protease domain; with weaker cross‑activation on analogous disulfide‑stabilized helix–loop–β segments in other protease catalytic domains (e.g., snake‑venom M12B metalloproteinases).

sae_features.label: S1 IVGG and CUB/EGF
sae_features.description: Extracellular “complement/coagulation-type” protease architecture: the feature marks the N-terminal activation segment of trypsin-like (S1) serine protease catalytic domains (canonical IVGG/IVGGT start) and, more broadly, the β-strand cores of accessory CUB and EGF-like repeats that commonly flank these proteases (and related pseudo-/non‑S1 protease mosaics).

sae_features.label: Serine protease His–Asp segment
sae_features.description: Conserved H–D interstitial segment of serine endopeptidase catalytic domains: an extended loop/β‑strand block that lies immediately after the catalytic histidine and before the catalytic aspartate in the charge‑relay system, shaping the active‑site cleft and substrate pocket. The signal is strongest in trypsin/chymotrypsin-like Peptidase S1 domains but generalizes to the analogous region in other serine protease clans, and is present in active enzymes, zymogens, and pseudoproteases.

sae_features.label: Serine protease processing boundaries
sae_features.description: Protease-domain processing segments flanking chymotrypsin-fold serine proteases, especially trypsin-like (S1) enzymes: disordered linkers and N-termini at zymogen activation/processing sites (e.g., heavy–light chain junctions and the newly exposed IVGG-like start of the mature chain), with occasional recognition of analogous boundary/regulatory elements within related serine proteases.

sae_features.label: Trypsin-like serine protease domain
sae_features.description: Chymotrypsin/trypsin-fold serine protease catalytic domain (Clan PA; S1 family and close homologs), recognized at the level of the conserved protease core in both zymogen and active forms—capturing the fold’s glycine‑rich loops (e.g., GxWG and the region surrounding the catalytic Ser) and adjacent sequence features—across secreted and type II transmembrane enzymes, including catalytically impaired homologs

sae_features.label: S1 protease post-serine region
sae_features.description: C‑terminal subregion of chymotrypsin/trypsin‑like serine protease catalytic domains (Peptidase S1 and related clan PA): the post‑nucleophile stretch immediately following the catalytic Ser (or homologous Cys), enriched for glycine repeats and a characteristic W–G–x–G–C motif, spanning the final loops/β‑strands and terminal α‑helix of the protease domain

sae_features.label: Asp-centered serine protease core
sae_features.description: Conserved catalytic-core segment of clan PA serine endopeptidases (trypsin/chymotrypsin-like), focusing on the β-strand/loop neighborhood that harbors the catalytic Asp of the His–Asp–Ser charge-relay within Peptidase S1/S6/HtrA domains; the feature marks the catalytic chain of secreted or membrane-tethered zymogens across diverse taxa and often includes nearby Gly/Ser-rich elements of the active-site cleft.

sae_features.label: Clan PA nucleophile loop
sae_features.description: Catalytic nucleophile loop of clan PA (chymotrypsin-like) proteases—recognition of the short glycine-rich segment immediately around the catalytic Ser or Cys (e.g., GxDSGG/GNSGG/CQGDSGG in S1A/S1C serine proteases and GxCG in viral 3C proteases) and the immediately following beta-strand; also fires on protease-like pseudoenzymes that retain this loop architecture.
